title: Switching to peat-free and plastic-reduced gardening supplies
subtitle: Replacing peat compost, plastic pots, and synthetic feeds with lower-impact alternatives that perform at the same level
excerpt: Peat extraction drains carbon-storing bogs at a rate that home gardening perpetuates even now that professional horticulture has largely switched. This is the practical swap guide for compost, pots, labels, and feeds: which peat-free alternatives match the job, which do not, and where bulk buying reduces plastic packaging.
sourceNotes: DEFRA 'Responsible use of peat in horticulture' consultation 2022. RHS trials of peat-free growing media 2021-2023. WRAP 'Plastic in horticulture' evidence review. Garden Organic peat-free factsheets.

glossaryTerms.definition: A growing medium formulated without extracted peat. Common bases include coir (coconut husk fibre), wood fibre, composted bark, green compost, and biochar. Performance varies by crop type; seed-raising and ericaceous mixes require specific formulations.

An acidic growing medium (pH 4.5 to 5.5) for acid-loving plants such as blueberries, heathers, and rhododendrons. Peat-free ericaceous options use composted pine bark or acidified coir to achieve the target pH.

content.text: Peat bogs store twice as much carbon per hectare as forest. UK amateur gardeners buy approximately 50 million bags of peat-containing compost per year. The swap to

is not technically difficult but does require matching the right product to the job, because early peat-free mixes had poor structure for seed raising. Current formulations from reputable brands perform comparably for most uses when managed correctly.

General potting and containers: coir-based or wood-fibre mixes work well. They dry faster than peat, so water container plants more often. Add biochar or water-retaining granules for pots in full sun.

Seed raising: fine-textured peat-free seed composts are now widely available. Avoid chunky bark mixes for seed raising as the air pockets dry the surface too quickly. Look for products described as fine-grade or seed-and-cutting specific.
based on pine bark is available and performs reliably. Check the pH on the bag; it should be 4.5 to 5.5. Some budget ericaceous peat-free mixes drift toward pH 6 within a season.

Soil amendment and raised beds: home compost or purchased green compost (made from council food and garden waste collections) replaces peat entirely. Green compost is nutrient-rich; use at 30 to 50% of the bed volume mixed with topsoil.

Terracotta and wood: both durable and reusable for decades. Terracotta dries faster than plastic, which benefits plants prone to overwatering. Wooden seed trays are harder-wearing than the flimsy single-use plastic trays sold with bedding plants.
Paper and coir pots: biodegradable and plant-directly for seedlings with sensitive roots. Not suitable for longer-term container growing as they break down within a season.

Labels: cut white yoghurt pot lids or thick milk containers into strips; these last multiple seasons, can be washed and reused, and replace bamboo-skewer-and-sticker combinations. Write with a waterproof permanent marker.

Liquid feed: compost leachate from an active heap, diluted nettle or comfrey tea, and diluted worm casting liquid are all zero-packaging plant feeds. Make comfrey or nettle tea by packing a bucket with cut leaves, topping with water, and leaving for two to three weeks. Dilute 10 to 1 before applying to avoid leaf scorch.

Pest deterrents: copper tape, fine mesh netting, and companion planting cut dependency on packaged pesticides. Slug pellets based on ferric phosphate are wildlife-safe and biodegradable. Metaldehyde products are now banned in the UK.
Reducing packaging by buying in bulk

A 70-litre bag of peat-free compost contains the same material as five 10-litre bags with 80% less plastic by weight. Garden centres sell large bags; many allow collection of bulk loose compost by trailer or car boot into reusable sacks. Co-ordinating a bulk buy with neighbours is the most efficient route if storage space is limited.
